repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem
On Windows and macOS, entries in `oldfiles` may differ only in case, but actually refer to the same file.

## Solution
Previously, deduplication used `Set_cstr_t`. We can add a similar `Set_path_t` that performs path-aware comparisons, ignoring case differences. On Windows, it could also ignore path separator differences, and treat `C:\foo` and `\foo` as the same path when the current drive is `C:`.

close #25033

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
    skip(not is_os('win') and not is_os('mac'), 'N/A: applies to case-insensitive systems')
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -301,6 +302,28 @@ describe('ShaDa support code', function()
     )
     api.nvim_set_option_value('shada', '', {})
   end)
+
+  it('deduplicates items on case-insensitive systems', function()
+    skip(not is_os('win') and not is_os('mac'), 'N/A: appleis to case-insensitive systems')

comment_data.body: needs a docstring calling out the key idea(s)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -42,6 +45,31 @@ static inline uint32_t hash_cstr_t(const char *s)
 
 #define equal_cstr_t strequal
 
+static inline uint32_t hash_path_t(const char *p)

comment_data.body:
is there a reason not to use `path_full_compare` or something like that? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/5d85669a33e10f1f156b086562458cbbc8054438/src/nvim/path.c#L57

or `path_fnamecmp` : https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/5d85669a33e10f1f156b086562458cbbc8054438/src/nvim/path.c#L352

no race condition
```suggestion
    -- Both filenames appear in shada file, but iteration order is unspecified.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -301,6 +302,28 @@ describe('ShaDa support code', function()
     )
     api.nvim_set_option_value('shada', '', {})
   end)
+
+  it('deduplicates items on case-insensitive systems', function()
+    skip(not is_os('win') and not is_os('mac'), 'N/A: applies to case-insensitive systems')
+    local file = dirname .. '/nonexist/dir/héllo'
+    nvim_command('e ' .. file)
+    file = api.nvim_buf_get_name(0)
+    feed('i1<Esc>')
+    nvim_command('wshada! ' .. dirshada)
+    nvim_command('bw!')
+    local upper = fn.toupper(file)
+    nvim_command('e ' .. upper)
+    feed('i123<Esc>')
+    nvim_command('mark a')
+    nvim_command('wshada ' .. dirshada)
+    nvim_command('bw!')
+    nvim_command('rshada! ' .. dirshada)
+    local oldfiles = api.nvim_get_vvar('oldfiles')
+    eq(1, #oldfiles)
+    -- Both filenames appear in shada file, but their order may be
+    -- nondeterministic due to race conditions.

comment_data.body:
just change the assert to `eq(2,...)` instead of skipping the entire test?

~~or, maybe setting the 'fileignorecase' option allows the test to work on case-sensitive platforms?~~ (No, need to gate it on `CASE_INSENSITIVE_FILENAME` instead of 'fileignorecase' because the shada data may have been written at any time in the past with a different 'fileignorecase' setting.)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -301,6 +302,28 @@ describe('ShaDa support code', function()
     )
     api.nvim_set_option_value('shada', '', {})
   end)
+
+  it('deduplicates items on case-insensitive systems', function()
+    skip(not is_os('win') and not is_os('mac'), 'N/A: applies to case-insensitive systems')

comment_data.body: can't use `path_fnamecmp` because 'fileignorecase' at time of shada write may differ when shada is loaded later. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40013#discussion_r3320031883

comment_regular_data.body: Pretty sure that case insensitivity is filesystem based, not OS based (although it is the *default* filesystem on macOS). Not sure if that should be handled or not.

> Pretty sure that case insensitivity is filesystem based, not OS based (although it is the _default_ filesystem on macOS). 

GPT gave me a similar heads-up yesterday as well, but I haven't dug deeper into it. Also, case conversion seems pretty complex, and I haven't fully wrapped my head about it. My original idea was to keep the behavior here consisten with file editing, i.e. `CASE_INSENSITIVE_FILENAME `, which could handle common cases.

As for filesystem-specific differences, I don't really have a good idea yet – maybe this needs an option? idk. Or maybe it's fine to just leave it as-is and close this PR? :D

> My original idea was to keep the behavior here consisten with file editing, i.e. `CASE_INSENSITIVE_FILENAME `, which could handle common cases.

that seems fine. 

it's true that the decision is filesystem-specific, but that's a bigger question that is rather out of scope for this PR. Vim's current answer for that is the `'fileignorecase'` option.

> // - fold '\\' -> '/' (path_fnamencmp treats them as equal) path separators are always `/` here, since we already call `TO_SLASH` in `shada_read_next_item`. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/edcaf1887ae60d78526abe73bd536af8ccfc2310/src/nvim/shada.c#L3274-L3275 Additionally, I tried 2 approaches – 1) always allocates and lowercases the path, 2) uses `set_path_t`. I'm not sure about the performance impact yet, and I plan to test it when I get some time. Hopefully this PR won’t noticeably affect startup time. > // - drop a leading drive letter "[A-Za-z]:" so that "C:\foo" and "\foo" land in the same bucket Interesting. I envisioned this comparison could be optimized after path normalization work is done (e.g. stripping `//./` and `//?/`), but I did not realize the `C:\foo` vs `\foo` case. 😂 BTW, `path_fnamencmp` has a small bug that probably needs to be addressed first–it can get stuck in the loop:

I ran two somewhat extreme benchmarks. Both `ascii.shada` and `utf.shada` contain 500 file paths, and each path has 28 local mark entries and 100 change entries. Would results like the following be considered acceptable? ```pwsh > hyperfine --warmup 3 'nvim.exe --clean -i utf.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q' 'D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/bin/nvim.exe --clean -i utf.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q' Benchmark 1: nvim.exe --clean -i utf.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q Time (mean ± σ): 128.4 ms ± 4.0 ms [User: 66.6 ms, System: 70.8 ms] Range (min … max): 121.5 ms … 136.4 ms 20 runs Benchmark 2: D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/bin/nvim.exe --clean -i utf.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q Time (mean ± σ): 604.0 ms ± 16.1 ms [User: 538.4 ms, System: 68.4 ms] Range (min … max): 582.8 ms … 637.0 ms 10 runs Summary nvim.exe --clean -i utf.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q ran 4.70 ± 0.19 times faster than D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/bin/nvim.exe --clean -i utf.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q > hyperfine --warmup 3 'nvim.exe --clean -i ascii.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q' 'D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/bin/nvim.exe --clean -i ascii.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q' Benchmark 1: nvim.exe --clean -i ascii.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q Time (mean ± σ): 112.7 ms ± 3.3 ms [User: 58.1 ms, System: 61.4 ms] Range (min … max): 107.1 ms … 120.0 ms 24 runs Benchmark 2: D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/bin/nvim.exe --clean -i ascii.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q Time (mean ± σ): 414.1 ms ± 3.6 ms [User: 333.8 ms, System: 77.5 ms] Range (min … max): 406.5 ms … 420.2 ms 10 runs Summary nvim.exe --clean -i ascii.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q ran 3.68 ± 0.11 times faster than D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/bin/nvim.exe --clean -i ascii.shada --headless +"set shada=" +q ```
lua script for shada

```lua it('test shada', function () local function make_shada(p, name) n.clear() local dir = 'd:/a/neovim/test/issue-25033-oldfiles' local lower = ('%s/%s'):format(dir, p) local upper = fn.toupper(lower) local lines = {} for l = 1, 500 do lines[l] = tostring(l) end nvim_command([[set shada=!,'1000,<50,s10,h,r/tmp/,r/private/]]) for i = 1, 500 do local fname = (i % 2 == 0) and ('%s/FOO%s.MD'):format(upper, i) or ('%s/foo%s.md'):format(lower, i) nvim_command(('e %s'):format(fname)) api.nvim_buf_set_lines(0, 0, -1, false, lines) for j = 1, 100 do api.nvim_buf_set_lines(0, j, j + 1, false, { ('changed_'):format(j) }) end for c = 97, 122 do nvim_command(('mark %s'):format(string.char(c))) end print(i) end nvim_command(('wshada! %s/%s'):format(dir, name)) end make_shada('aofieonavbaeufhadkdjfoivbqerbqebvvoaidjfenfoij', 'ascii.shada') make_shada('straße_café_äpfel_über_istanbul_ΑΒΓ_моска_東京', 'utf.shada') end) ```
That does look a bit slow, but I'm not worried about performance. We can always fix that later after we get correct behavior + test coverage

title: fix(shada): prevent case-duplicate oldfiles
